rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERTIE VICTORIA FOSTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent died on 5/1/15, so the first two-year accounting is now very delinquent. Preston Segura is also entitled to all of the AT&T stock, but that was not part of the request for preliminary distribution (only $125,000 in cash was ordered to each residuary beneficiary on 12/17/19). Preston Segura and Carlton Jackson are entitled to the residue of the estate (aside from the real property and the $100,000 specifically devised to the co-executors). There was $687,011.88 in cash inventoried. There should also be "rents and royalties" from the Louisiana properties (according to the co-executors' own allegation in the predeceased husband's estate) that were being paid to this decedent and reportedly now to this estate -- presumably since 2017, when Letters Testamentary were issued to these co-executors. No one representing Preston Segura's interest was party to the settlement agreement approved on 1/30/17, which affected the residue of this estate. His one-half of the residue of this estate should, therefore, not be affected by the settlement agreement. Still nothing has been filed to indicate whose children the two nephews are, despite the Court's instructions at the 6/8/22 hearing and again at the hearing on 8/8/22 that a supplement be filed to address this. If the payment to Alice Livingston has been made pursuant to the settlement agreement, no receipt has been filed. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANE FRANCES WALSH REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] No copy of the proof of service has been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The I&A filed on 2/1/22 showed cash totaling $34,942.53, but Attachment 1 showed the balance in the Bank of America account as $41,945.13. (Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be checked or answered when a Probate Referee has been appointed.) The legal fees and costs are not supported by a fee declaration with time sheets and detailed descriptions of the costs. If time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. (This Court does not allow "support staff" to bill for services unless they are qualified paralegals.) Legal fees and costs reflected on the disbursements schedule for the second accounting also are not supported. Assets on Hand as of 7/2/21, reportedly on Schedule E, do not agree with the figures on the Schedule of Assets on Hand as of Close of Accounting Period. None of the schedules show account numbers or the specific assets held in the brokerage account. A lump sum for a brokerage account is not sufficient. The Portsmouth brokerage statement ending 6/30/21 reflects total shares of some of the holdings that differ from the shares indicated on the I&A filed on 2/1/22 (see, for example, Raytheon Technologies, Urban Edge, Blackrock Enhanced Intl, and Nuveen California Municipal Value Fund). Court-conformed accountings are cash accountings. There should be gains and losses schedules if securities were sold (not "removal" of carry values) and an informational schedule showing dividends reinvested. Why so many "dividend reinvestment" fees? Reimbursements to any individual, especially a fiduciary, must clearly show who the original payee was. There was no need to file two accountings for a trust or an I&A for the date of death values. In any event, only the beginning values and ending values need to be verified by original statements. Whenever final distribution is requested, the specific assets to be distributed to each beneficiary must be set forth, not just "equally" or percentages. Some of the pages of the Portsmouth brokerage statement for the period ending 6/30/21 are missing (pp. 29 and 31 and 33-43). (And the pages were collated oddly.) The statement indicates that there was also cash, but only the cash in the Bank of America account was inventoried. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/22/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEXANDER WITHERILL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/21/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice was not given to the CCSF, who requested special notice and has not yet withdrawn the request. For future reference/incorporation in a petition for final distribution: Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) requires notice if an heir or beneficiary is currently or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. A petition for final distribution must state the DATE on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] Courtesy copies of the proof of service of notice to the CCSF and any other papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAR WAI LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Succession T Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) A copy of the supplement filed on 04/07/2022 has not been served on the decedent's former spouse, as required by CRC 7.53(b) & Probate Code section 851(a) (or if it has been served, no proof of service has been filed). 2) The acknowledgment of receipt of a copy of the petition and notice of the hearing signed by the former spouse was not on the mandatory Judicial Council form POS-015 for service of process under CCP section 415.30. 3) The stipulated divorce judgment did not divide the decedent's Roth IRA as community property, nor did the judgment confirm the Roth IRA as the decedent's separate property. The extent of the decedent's interest in the Roth IRA, to which the automatic revocation under section 5040(a) applies in favor of the estate as the alternate designated beneficiary, has not been determined by the family court retaining jurisdiction to characterize and divide omitted assets. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN ANDREW SCULLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Cynthia Scully, the decedent's surviving spouse, has not waived her higher priority for appointment under Probate Code section 8463. The decedent's father nominated the petitioner, but the father is not a person otherwise entitled to appointment within the meaning of section 8465(a)(1), as he is not an heir and has no priority under section 8462(a). 2) If the surviving spouse failed to claim her priority for appointment, the San Francisco Public Administrator would have higher priority than the petitioner. No notice has been given to the San Francisco Public Administrator. 3) No guardian of the estate (who would have higher priority than the petitioner and surviving spouse under section 8464) has been appointed for any minor child of the decedent. 4) The Court is to determine whether a guardian ad litem should be appointed for the decedent's minor children at this stage of the proceeding. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL BARBARA HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 9/9/99. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 11/6/23. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 11/6/23, no appearance will be required on that date. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN L. VENTURA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/9/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately with a cover sheet, conventionally, and confidentially. No courtesy copy of any paper other than the petition was received by the assigned examiner. [[LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Courtesy copies of the form DE-147S and other papers filed in support of the petition must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/31/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
